summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 10 (IMVT-KRUS)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: Definitive merger-arb: Western Union acquiring at $16.00 all-cash (~$500M); shareholders already approved, closing mid-2026. Clean dated arb but simple and spread likely thin.

Live complicated merger-arb: Steel Partners non-binding proposal for 51%; independent committee formed + legal/financial advisors retained, evaluating alternatives (~$1.1B / 25% premium reports). Process ongoing, not consummated.

EMEA Packaging spin-off announced Jan 29 2026, targeted ~12-15mo (H1-27) dual NY/London listing with IP retaining a stake; GCF fluff-pulp divestiture just closed Jan 2026 — clean radical-simplification special-sit.
Formal strategic review (sale/merger) launched Apr 30 2026 under activist Irenic Capital pressure; live process, no fixed timeline caps it.
Zanidatamab 1L HER2+ GEA PDUFA Aug 25 2026 - real dated binary, but incremental on a $10B+ base, modest asymmetry.
Definitive all-cash takeout by Trian/General Catalyst at $52/sh (Victory withdrew competing bid); on track to close mid-2026 — live merger-arb with dated close.

Board-approved tax-free spin of Mission Technology Solutions (SpinCo) targeted ~mid/late-2026 (Jan-4-2027), PLUS activists Engine Capital (push full sale) & Irenic (push split) hardening the trigger. Dated structural catalyst.

Tax-free spin into Beverage Co. + Global Coffee Co. targeted operationally-ready by YE2026 (milestone/leverage-gated); JDE Peet's $18B acquisition closed ~Apr 2026 and now integrating. Spin is the live forward leg.
Board-approved tax-free separation into Global Taste Elevation Co. ($15.4B sales) and North American Grocery Co. ($10.4B), expected to close 2H 2026. Clean, dated, forward break-up.

Kimberly-Clark acquiring Kenvue $48.7B (largest US staples deal); shareholders approved Jan 2026, now in FTC 'second request' with baby/feminine-care overlap risk + Tylenol litigation, close targeted 2H 2026. Classic antitrust-risk arb (acquirer side).
Zenkuda DR Ph3 positive; forward dated binaries: DAYBREAK wAMD readout 3Q26 + PEAK MESI 4Q26, with potential BLAs to follow. Real dated catalysts but no clear merger-arb-style mispriced asymmetry.

verified.catalyst: Western Union is acquiring Intermex (IMXI) for $16.00/share all-cash (~$500M) under a signed, shareholder-approved merger agreement, with only final state money-transmitter and Bank of Spain approvals left before close.
verified.dated_milestone: Final regulatory clearances (remaining US state(s) + Bank of Spain) and deal close; original outside date ~May 11, 2026 has lapsed (subject to automatic extensions), targeted close Q2 2026 (by ~June 30, 2026).
verified.instrument: stock (long IMXI common into $16.00 cash close); merger-arb, not optionable-attractive given thin upside
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Western Union), G2 (concrete commitment: signed all-cash agreement, shareholders approved Dec 9 2025, HSR expired Oct 6 2025), G3 (specific unpriced figure: $16.00 fixed price vs $14.95 spot = ~$1.05 / ~7.0% gross spread)
verified.edge: M — clean dated cash arb with ~7% gross to $16.00, but spread is unusually WIDE for a 'thin' arb, signaling real completion/timing risk: original ~May 11 2026 outside date has already passed without close, ~1-4 US state licenses + Bank of Spain still pending, and NYC Mayor Mamdani is publicly pressuring NY state to block the deal.
verified.primary_source: IMXI Q1 2026 10-Q via StockTitan (filed ~May 2026); WU/Intermex deal PR (BusinessWire, Aug 10 2025); PYMNTS (June 2026) on NYC Mayor opposition; FMP quote $14.95 (June 6 2026)
verified.kill_fact: Core deal CONFIRMED from primary sources (IMXI Q1-2026 10-Q + IR/WU press releases): Western Union acquiring IMXI at $16.00/sh all-cash (~$500M); IMXI stockholders APPROVED at a special meeting Dec 9, 2025; HSR waiting period expired with no second request; 51 of 52 US states/territories have approved/non-objected with only ONE state (New York) pending; and ALL international money-transmission regulators have already approved. Company is "working towards consummation in Q2 2026." Price $14.95 vs $16.00 = ~$1.05 / ~7% gross spread. BUT two LINE facts are WRONG: (1) The claim the "original ~May 11 2026 outside date lapsed without close" is false/misleading — the May 11, 2026 outside date AUTO-EXTENDS to Aug 10, 2026 and then Nov 10, 2026 if money-transmitter restraints persist; it did not lapse, it was contractually built to extend for exactly this pending-state scenario. (2) "Bank of Spain approval left" is UNSUPPORTED — the 10-Q states all international money-transmission regulators are already done; the only real outstanding approval is NY DFS. The elevated spread is driven NOT by a lapsed deadline but by NY/DFS completion risk: NYC Mayor Mamdani sent a letter (~late Apr/May 2026, reported May 13-14 2026 by Semafor/American Banker/PYMNTS) urging NY DFS to block the deal on remittance-competition grounds — and NY is precisely the one pending state. Directional thesis (legitimate ACTIVE merger-arb, ~7% spread reflecting genuine residual completion/timing risk, score ~6) holds; the supporting specifics are corrected.
verified.analysis: This is a genuine, specific, dated forward merger-arb: WU/$16.00 all-cash, shareholder- and board-approved, HSR-cleared, with the bulk of state and international regulators done. However it is NOT the 'simple, thin-spread' clean arb the triage assumed — at $14.95 the ~7% gross spread is wide for a near-closing deal, reflecting that the original ~May 11 2026 outside date lapsed without close and that final NY/state + Bank of Spain approvals plus fresh political opposition (Mayor Mamdani) inject real timing-and-completion risk. Legitimate ACTIVE arb with a concrete next catalyst (final clearances + Q2-2026 close), but the elevated spread caps conviction at score 6.

verified.catalyst: Steel Partners' non-binding $18.00/share proposal for 51% of InMode is DEAD — the independent transaction committee rejected all final proposals as inadequate and discontinued the process on Feb 23, 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: None forward; terminal event already passed — committee concluded/discontinued review on 2026-02-23. No pending deal, vote, or next date.
verified.instrument: none (no live arb spread; equity is now a standalone turnaround story, not an arb)
verified.bloom_gates: none (G1 counterparty walked; no G2 commitment toward a transaction; no G3 unpriced forward figure)
verified.edge: L — fully fired/public; outcome (no deal) was disclosed Feb 23, 2026 and re-rated; nothing forward to capture.
verified.primary_source: StockTitan / InMode 6-K "Conclusion of Independent Transaction Committee" — committee discontinued process, dated 2026-02-23; corroborated by FMP stock news (no deal-related items Apr-Jun 2026; only standalone ops/leadership news).
verified.analysis: The triage flag is stale by ~3.5 months: it describes a "live, ongoing" merger-arb process, but InMode's independent committee rejected all proposals (incl. Steel Partners' $18.00/sh for 51%, ~29% premium to unaffected 1/23/26 price) as inadequate and discontinued the review on Feb 23, 2026. No transaction is pending, no shareholder vote, no consummation path, and the company has since pivoted to standalone strategy (new Chairman Dr. Shlomo Nass + CFO Moshik Itzkovich appointed May 20, 2026; Q1 2026 revenue $82M, FY guide $365-375M reiterated). With the deal terminated and re-rated, there is no dated forward catalyst to underwrite — tier NONE.

verified.catalyst: International Paper is spinning off its combined EMEA Packaging business into a separately, dual NYSE/LSE-listed company (IP retaining a 20% stake), a board-approved radical-simplification separation targeted for completion within 12-15 months of the Jan 29 2026 announcement (i.e. H1-2027).
verified.dated_milestone: Separation completion targeted H1-2027 (12-15mo from Jan 29 2026 announce); next checkpoint = Q2-2026 earnings (~late Jul 2026) for Form 10 progress / firmer date.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named structure: dual NYSE/LSE listing + IP retains 20% stake, CEO Tim Nicholls) and G2 (board-approved 12-15mo completion commitment, confirmed on-track at Q1-2026 print). G3 only partial — EMEA standalone ~$900M-1B EBITDA target is disclosed but largely in consensus.
verified.edge: M — real special-sit (radical simplification, sum-of-parts re-rate, separation-driven index/forced-selling flow), but widely covered and consensus-known, not a hidden/unpriced figure; milestone ~9-12mo out with containerboard-pricing/execution risk; stock $33.61 vs 52wk-high $56.13 reflects cut guidance + impairments, not the spin optionality yet.
verified.primary_source: International Paper press release "International Paper to Create Two Independent Public Companies," Jan 29 2026 (internationalpaper q4web IR); corroborated by Q1-2026 earnings update via Packaging Dive, ~Apr 2026.
verified.analysis: Catalyst is fully corroborated and clean: IP's Jan 29 2026 board-approved plan spins the merged DS Smith+IP EMEA Packaging unit into a dual NYSE/LSE-listed standalone with IP keeping a 20% stake for 12-18mo, and the Q1-2026 (Apr 2026) update confirmed "meaningful progress" and on-track within the 12-15mo window — so it is real, named, dated and forward (not fired). It clears G1/G2 strongly but it is a high-profile, well-telegraphed large-cap separation rather than a hidden currently-unpriced figure, and the hard date is still ~H1-2027 with macro/pricing execution risk, so the unpriced edge is moderate. WATCH (score 5): legitimate dated forward spin-off, but escalate to ACTIVE only on a concrete Form 10 filing / firm distribution date or a sum-of-parts dislocation at a lower price.

verified.catalyst: Board-run formal strategic review (sale/merger/combination) launched Apr 30 2026 under Irenic Capital activist pressure, advised by Goldman Sachs, with no deal yet announced.
verified.dated_milestone: Review live since Apr 30 2026 with NO fixed timeline; sell-side base case is a deal within 3-4 months (~Aug-Sep 2026); nearest events are the May 20 2026 annual meeting (passed) and June 16 2026 Truist MedTech conference (not a deal catalyst).
verified.bloom_gates: G3 partial only — specific unpriced figure exists (analyst fair value $110-125/share, ~13-14x fwd EBITDA precedent vs ~10x current, ~20-40% upside from ~$92); FAILS G1 (no named counterparty/bidder) and G2 (board explicitly states no fixed timeline and no guaranteed transaction).
verified.edge: M — real idiosyncratic event with activist forcing function (cooperation agreement + Aaron Kapito board seat) and Goldman engaged, but process is open-ended (no timeline), no named bidder, and ~5% already re-rated on the May 13 buyout upgrade, so partial repricing has begun.
verified.primary_source: Integer Holdings 8-K / GlobeNewsWire "Integer Announces Strategic Review to Maximize Stockholder Value," Apr 30 2026; corroborated by Seeking Alpha "Rare Public Asset Soon To Be Sold," May 21 2026 (timeline + $110-125 target) and Fool "Why Integer Stock Leaped More Than 5%," May 13 2026 (buyout-driven analyst upgrade).
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs PRIMARY sources. Strategic review launched Apr 30 2026 (8-K + GlobeNewsWire press release): board weighing 'sale, merger, or strategic business combination vs standalone execution.' Goldman Sachs financial advisor (Davis Polk legal) confirmed. Irenic Capital cooperation agreement (dated Mar 9 2026, DEF 14A/8-K) with board seats (Flanagan + Kapito, eff. Mar 12) confirmed. Catalyst is FORWARD/not-fired: no definitive agreement and no named bidder as of latest news (Jun 3-4 2026 = routine Truist conf invite + Zacks outlook). Spot $91.98 confirmed (52wk hi $123.78). Valuation gap independently corroborated: Seeking Alpha (May 21) ~10x EBITDA, $110-125 FV, 13-14x CDMO precedents; GuruFocus GF Value $117.20. Company disclaims fixed timeline + no named bidder, so gated to ACTIVE-modest; score 6 well-calibrated. Minor: 'soon to be sold' partly consensus/story.
verified.analysis: ITGR's board formally launched a strategic review on Apr 30 2026 (confirmed via GlobeNewsWire press release and 8-K) explicitly weighing a sale/merger against standalone execution, driven by activist Irenic Capital, which holds a cooperation agreement and a board seat; Goldman Sachs is advising. The catalyst is genuine, dated, forward (no deal/no definitive counterparty yet) and idiosyncratic, with a clear valuation gap (~10x EBITDA vs 13-14x CDMO precedents, ~$110-125 fair value vs ~$92 spot). It is gated below conviction because the company explicitly disclaims any fixed timeline and there is no named bidder, so it satisfies only a soft G3 (specific unpriced figure) and fails the hard G1/G2 tests, warranting ACTIVE-but-modest sizing rather than a high score.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision on Ziihera (zanidatamab) combination sBLA for first-line HER2+ locally advanced/metastatic gastroesophageal adenocarcinoma (GEA), under Priority Review.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA action date Aug 25, 2026 (sBLA accepted Apr 2026, Priority Review).
verified.instrument: equity (JAZZ); long-dated Sep/Oct 2026 calls if expressing the binary, but premium rich given run-up
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific quantified figure: UBS ~$3.1B peak Ziihera sales / 45% peak penetration; Jazz guides program peak >$2B) tied to a dated decision — but the figure is the consensus thesis, not currently-unpriced.
verified.edge: L — the de-risking catalyst (HERIZON-GEA Phase 3 "unprecedented" >2yr median OS) already fired and the stock already re-rated; UBS PT $307 explicitly cites that data.
verified.primary_source: Jazz Pharmaceuticals / PRNewswire & StockTitan, "FDA Acceptance and Priority Review of sBLA for Ziihera Combinations in 1L HER2+ GEA," PDUFA Aug 25, 2026 (Apr 2026); peak-sales/valuation context: UBS PT reset to $307 (TheStreet, 2026), ~$3.1B peak Ziihera.
verified.analysis: Real, dated, forward binary: confirmed Aug 25, 2026 PDUFA for Ziihera 1L HER2+ GEA, accepted Apr 2026, decision ~2.5 months out and not yet fired. But the asymmetry is poor — JAZZ trades $238.57 near its 52-wk high of $243.32 (+41% above the $169 200-day avg) precisely because the supportive Phase 3 data is already public and analysts (UBS PT $307) have already priced the GEA expansion; this is a high-probability incremental 1L approval on a ~$15B-cap, diversified-revenue company, so the edge from the PDUFA itself is largely spent. Clears NONE (genuine dated forward catalyst) but caps at WATCH given the modest, already-harvested asymmetry.

verified.catalyst: Definitive all-cash takeout of Janus Henderson by Trian Fund Management + General Catalyst at $52.00/share, shareholder-approved (99.7% of votes cast), with Victory Capital's competing bid rejected as non-actionable.
verified.dated_milestone: Expected close mid-2026; next dated trigger June 30, 2026 (if close slips past it, JHG may pay a $1.00/sh quarterly dividend until completion).
verified.instrument: stock (long common into close; ~$0.24/0.46% gross spread vs $52, plus optional $1.00/sh dividend accrual if regulatory delay)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties Trian + General Catalyst), G2 (signed/amended definitive merger agreement, shareholder vote passed Apr 16 2026), G3 ($0.24 spread + contingent $1.00/sh quarterly dividend past Jun 30 — currently-unpriced incremental cash)
verified.edge: M — deal is signed, shareholder-approved, and competing bid rejected, so close-probability is high; but spread is thin (~0.46% gross) so absolute edge is small and concentrated in (a) timely close and (b) the optional $1.00/sh dividend if regulatory/client-consent delays push past Jun 30.
verified.primary_source: Businesswire / Janus Henderson IR: amended merger at $52.00/sh (Mar 24, 2026); shareholder approval announcement (Apr 16, 2026); FMP quote JHG $51.76 (Jun 5, 2026)
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently confirmed vs PRIMARY sources (JHG IR/BusinessWire/SEC DEFA14A + Victory 8-K): $52.00/sh all-cash Trian+General Catalyst (amended up); shareholder vote 99.7% of votes cast on Apr 16, 2026; $1.00/sh quarterly dividend if not closed by Jun 30, 2026 due to regulatory delay; price $51.76 (FMP quote exact) = $0.24/0.46% gross spread. Forward, NOT fired: deal pending mid-2026 close; June 2, 2026 Trian affiliate (Jupiter Borrower) launched cash tender for JHG senior notes = active close/financing mechanics. CORRECTION: line says Victory bid 'rejected as non-actionable' but Victory has since FORMALLY WITHDRAWN its proposal (Victory 8-K) — even more de-risked, favorable to thesis. Edge real but thin: ~0.46% gross absolute spread is the binding constraint, not catalyst doubt; size modest. Residual risk = customary regulatory approvals + client consents only.
verified.analysis: Real, specific, dated forward merger-arb: amended definitive agreement at $52.00/share cash (Trian + General Catalyst), with 99.7% of votes cast in favor on Apr 16, 2026, and Victory Capital's rival proposal deemed non-actionable — materially de-risking the close. With JHG at $51.76, ~$0.24 (~0.46%) gross spread remains plus a contingent $1.00/sh quarterly dividend that becomes payable if the deal slips past June 30, 2026, which the consensus tape is not fully crediting. Remaining gates are customary regulatory approvals and client consents (close expected mid-2026); thin absolute spread is the main reason to size modestly rather than the catalyst being doubtful.

verified.catalyst: Board-approved tax-free spin of Mission Technology Solutions (SpinCo), with activists Engine Capital (~2%, pushing full sale at $48-55) and Irenic (pushing separation) hardening the trigger into a potential outright sale.
verified.dated_milestone: Separation targeted mid-to-late 2026 (official KBR guidance; Form 10 filing the next concrete gate). Triage's "Jan-4-2027" overshoots official window but remains forward/unfired.
verified.instrument: stock; long-dated calls viable given 35-55% gap to Engine's $48-55 sale floor and a hard mid/late-2026 completion catalyst
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: Engine Capital, Irenic Capital), G2 (concrete commitment: board unanimously approved tax-free spin), G3 (specific unpriced figure: Engine's $48-55/sh sale value vs $35.56 spot)
verified.edge: H — board-approved structural separation PLUS two named activists pushing for a richer outcome (full sale), with stock de-rated (UBS PT $36, prior $78->$65) so the ~35-55% upside to the sale floor is not priced; idiosyncratic, dated, and unfired.
verified.primary_source: KBR press release "Strategic Intent to Spin Off Mission Technology Solutions" (Sep 24, 2025, globenewswire/kbr.com) + Reuters/WSJ via Hedgeweek & Seeking Alpha on Engine Capital sale push (Apr 30, 2026); FMP quote $35.56 (Jun 5, 2026 close)
verified.kill_fact: LINE's completion-date correction is inverted/false: KBR's own definitive guidance (press release + GlobeNewswire Sep 24 2025 + Q1 FY2026 filings) targets spin completion Jan 4, 2027 (first business day of FY2027), NOT 'mid-to-late 2026.' Triage's 'Jan-4-2027' was correct; the LINE 'corrects' it backwards, mis-dating the catalyst ~6 months early and undermining the long-dated-calls timing thesis. Otherwise verified: price $35.56 (FMP live), 52-wk high $54.38, gap to floor +35-55%; Engine Capital ~2% urged full sale at $48-55 in a letter ~Apr 30 2026 (Ajdler), Irenic pushed separation since late-2024, KeyBanc reiterated amid activist push. Spin = board-approved strategic intent (final board approval still pending), forward and not fired. Score cut from 7.5: only the spin is base case; the $48-55 sale is an unagreed activist ask with no buyer, and the wrong date weakens the instrument rationale.
verified.analysis: KBR's board unanimously approved a tax-free spin of Mission Technology Solutions (announced Sep 24, 2025, completion targeted mid/late-2026), and the trigger has hardened: Engine Capital (~2% stake) publicly urged a full sale at $48-55/share on Apr 30, 2026, while Irenic continues pushing for separation — confirmed by KeyBanc/UBS coverage. At $35.56 (vs 52-wk high $54.38), the breakup/sale optionality is clearly unpriced after a sharp de-rating, giving real forward edge. Gap vs official guidance: triage cites "Jan-4-2027" but KBR's stated window is mid-to-late 2026; either way the catalyst is forward, dated, and not yet fired.

verified.catalyst: Tax-free spin separating KDP into two U.S.-listed companies, "Beverage Co." (CEO Tim Cofer) and "Global Coffee Co." (CEO Rafael Oliveira), following the now-closed ~$18B JDE Peet's acquisition.
verified.dated_milestone: Operational readiness to separate targeted by year-end 2026; official separation expected early 2027 (market/leverage-gated). Next checkpoint: YE2026 readiness; intervening Q2 2026 earnings ~late Jul 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete board-sanctioned commitment to a tax-free spin into two named entities with named CEOs and defined leverage targets); partial G1 (named principals/CEOs; Apollo+KKR $4.5B convert is financing, not the spin counterparty).
verified.edge: M-L — real, dated, forward, idiosyncratic spin (not macro), but well-telegraphed since the Aug-2025 deal announcement and largely anticipated; the big re-rating leg (JDE Peet's acquisition) already fired Apr 2026, and spin timing is soft/gated (slipped from YE2026 to early 2027, "exact timing yet to be determined," market-dependent), so limited unpriced upside and no hard catalyst date.
verified.primary_source: KDP press release "Keurig Dr Pepper Acquires JDE Peet's..." (Apr 1, 2026) + KDP Q1 2026 earnings call transcript (Apr 23, 2026) confirming early-2027 separation target; JDE Peet's delisted Euronext Apr 30, 2026.
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs primary sources. JDE Peet's acquisition CLOSED Apr 1 2026 (Q1 PR: 'Completed Acquisition of JDE Peet's on April 1'); JDE Peet's last trade Apr 29 / delisted Euronext Amsterdam Apr 30 2026. Spin into Beverage Co. (CEO Tim Cofer) and Global Coffee Co. (CEO Rafael Oliveira, named at Apr 1 close) is structurally committed; tax-free spin of Global Coffee Co. referenced in KDP financing release. Separation-leverage targets EXACT: BevCo ~3.5-4.0x, Coffee ~3.75-4.25x. Timing softened: operational readiness YE2026, actual separation 'early 2027,' market-conditions dependent (per Cofer / Q1 call) — no hard near-term date. Larger re-rating event (JDEP close) already FIRED; spin is genuine, dated, forward, non-macro but well-telegraphed and only partially unpriced. Tradeable: KDP NASDAQ, $30.53, ~$41.5B cap, ~9.7M ADV. Minor soft spot: primary releases reference tax-free spin but do not explicitly state both entities 'U.S.-listed' (BevCo is the remaining NASDAQ entity). Score 6 / ACTIVE upheld.
verified.analysis: The forward leg is genuine and structurally committed: KDP will spin into BevCo and Global Coffee Co., U.S.-listed, with named CEOs and explicit separation-leverage targets (BevCo 3.5-4x, Coffee 3.75-4.25x), so it clears G2 as a concrete, dated, forward, non-macro catalyst. However it is well-telegraphed and only partially unpriced — the JDE Peet's acquisition (the larger re-rating event) already closed ~Apr 1, 2026 and JDE Peet's delisted Apr 30, 2026, leaving the spin as the remaining but anticipated step. Timing is gated and has already softened (operational readiness YE2026, actual separation now "expected early 2027," market-conditions dependent), so it stays ACTIVE but at a moderate score with no hard near-term date.

verified.catalyst: The Sept 2025 plan to split Kraft Heinz into Global Taste Elevation Co. (~$15.4B sales) and North American Grocery Co. (~$10.4B) via tax-free spin-off was PAUSED by the board/new CEO on Feb 11, 2026, with completion now uncertain.
verified.dated_milestone: No live forward date. Original 2H-2026 close was withdrawn; Feb 11, 2026 pause stands; Q1 2026 10-Q (period ended Mar 28, 2026) says timing and whether it completes are uncertain. No Form 10 / record date / distribution date set.
verified.bloom_gates: none (G1 no counterparty - it is a spin not a sale; G2 the concrete commitment was explicitly paused, not affirmed; G3 no specific currently-priced/unpriced figure tied to a live trigger)
verified.edge: L - the only dated forward event (2H26 separation close) was paused Feb 11, 2026; what remains is a paused/uncertain process already absorbed, with the stock drifting lower (~$22.58 on 2026-06-05), so no unpriced forward break-up optionality.
verified.primary_source: Kraft Heinz Q1 2026 Form 10-Q (period ended 2026-03-28, filed ~Apr 2026) + Feb 11, 2026 announcement pausing the separation; corroborated by StockTitan KHC news (price $22.58 as of 2026-06-05).
verified.analysis: The triage premise is stale: the "board-approved, dated, forward break-up" was the Sept 2, 2025 announcement, but on Feb 11, 2026 new CEO Steve Cahillane and the board PAUSED the separation, reallocating ~$600M into the operating business, and the Q1 2026 10-Q explicitly states the timing and whether the split completes are uncertain (no Form 10, no record date, no distribution date). It therefore fails FORWARD/live and DATED: the only concrete milestone was withdrawn ~4 months ago and is already absorbed, with KHC drifting to ~$22.58 (2026-06-05) rather than re-rating on break-up optionality. Net: a paused/uncertain corporate action, not a real dated forward catalyst — tier NONE.

verified.catalyst: KMB's $48.7B acquisition of Kenvue ($3.50 cash + 0.14625 KMB/share) awaits FTC "second request" clearance with baby/feminine-care overlap and Tylenol-litigation risk, targeted to close 2H 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: FTC second-request review ongoing; no public decision deadline. Next hard date: deal close window 2H 2026 (no specific date set). Q2 earnings ~late July 2026 as interim check.
verified.instrument: stock (acquirer KMB); the arb binary actually sits on target KVUE
verified.bloom_gates: G1 pass (named counterparty Kenvue); G2 pass (definitive merger agreement, concrete $3.50 + 0.14625 exchange); G3 FAIL (no specific currently-unpriced figure on the acquirer side)
verified.edge: L — On the acquirer, no unpriced binary: KVUE deal value = $3.50 + 0.14625x$99.04 = ~$17.99 vs KVUE $17.71, a ~1.6% gross spread to a 2H-2026 close, i.e. market prices the deal as likely to close. KMB carries antitrust/integration RISK without a corresponding dated upside re-rate; already analyst-rerated (BofA Buy, $120 PO, "opportunistic ~14x EBITDA").
verified.primary_source: Kenvue Form 425 / KMB 8-K + CNBC (deal terms $3.50 + 0.14625, close 2H 2026; shareholder vote Jan 29, 2026); Proactive/BofA note (2026-05-14, $21.01/sh, ~14x EBITDA, $120 PO); FMP quotes 2026-06-06 (KMB $99.04, KVUE $17.71)
verified.analysis: Real, specific, named deal (G1/G2 satisfied) but it fails the strict forward/unpriced test on the acquirer side: the FTC second request has no public decision date and "2H 2026" is a window, not a dated milestone, so there is no concrete near-term trigger. The ~1.6% KVUE arb spread shows the deal is already priced to close, and KMB's June 6 +4.8% pop is staples/yield rotation (Cramer, 5%+ yield rebound), not a deal catalyst — the binary lives on KVUE, not KMB. Net: a complicated, largely-priced merger-arb with no dated, currently-unpriced figure in KMB's favor, so it lands WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Kodiak Sciences has two dated forward Phase 3 binary readouts — DAYBREAK (Zenkuda/KSI-501 in wet AMD) and PEAK (KSI-101 in macular edema secondary to inflammation) — both potentially leading to BLAs.
verified.dated_milestone: DAYBREAK wet AMD topline = September 2026 (3Q26); PEAK MESI topline = 4Q26 (next: Sept 2026)
verified.instrument: equity (KOD) or long-dated calls to express the binary; no merger-arb instrument exists
verified.bloom_gates: None cleanly met — no G1 (no named counterparty/acquirer), no G2 (no concrete contractual commitment), no clear G3 (no specific currently-unpriced figure the market is demonstrably mispricing; valuation is a clinical-probability bet, not an identifiable mispriced number)
verified.edge: L — dated and forward but it is a probabilistic binary clinical event, not a mispriced asymmetry; no edge over consensus on PoS, and the prior win (GLOW2) already re-rated the stock.
verified.primary_source: Kodiak Sciences Q1 2026 results, PRNewswire 2026-05 (DAYBREAK Sept 2026 / PEAK 4Q26 timing; $169.5M cash into 2027); GLOW2 positive topline PRNewswire 2026-03-26
verified.analysis: Triage was accurate: the Zenkuda diabetic-retinopathy Phase 3 win (GLOW2) already FIRED on 2026-03-26 and re-rated the stock ~19%, so it is not a forward edge. The genuine forward binaries — DAYBREAK wet AMD topline (Sept 2026) and PEAK KSI-101 MESI topline (4Q26), each with potential BLAs to follow — are real, specific and dated, but they are coin-flip clinical readouts with no named counterparty, no contractual commitment, and no identifiable currently-unpriced figure, so no Bloom gate is met. At $31.05 / $1.64B mkt cap (down 9% today, off the $39.70 50-day avg) the asymmetry is ordinary binary clinical risk, not a structurally mispriced setup, which keeps this WATCH rather than ACTIVE.
